schedules_text:
Gate A at (5,2): open exactly when (t mod 6) is in {0, 1, 5}. So it is open at t=0,1,5,6,7,11,... and closed at t=2,3,4,8,9,10,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Gate B at (4,8): open exactly when (t mod 6) is in {2, 3, 4}. So it is open at t=2,3,4,8,9,10,... and closed at t=0,1,5,6,7,11,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Guard P: walks a fixed cycle of length 8. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 8) as follows: t mod 8=0: (9,6); t mod 8=1: (9,7); t mod 8=2: (8,7); t mod 8=3: (7,7); t mod 8=4: (7,6); t mod 8=5: (7,7); t mod 8=6: (8,7); t mod 8=7: (9,7). This repeats forever.
Guard Q: walks a fixed cycle of length 6. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 6) as follows: t mod 6=0: (3,7); t mod 6=1: (3,6); t mod 6=2: (3,5); t mod 6=3: (2,5); t mod 6=4: (3,5); t mod 6=5: (3,6). This repeats forever.
parcels_text:
Parcel 1: pick it up at (3,7) (map glyph '1'), deliver it to (3,4) (map glyph 'x'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Parcel 2: pick it up at (9,9) (map glyph '2'), deliver it to (2,1) (map glyph 'y'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
